refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these were separated into distinct sub-FRs. Additionally, the FR was kept focused on 'what' the system must do (derive requirements) without implying how it should be done.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can identify and specify performance requirements based solely on the worksite mission context, independent of any recording or management functions.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall record the derived performance requirements.
The NGHE solution shall manage the recorded performance requirements over time.

candidate_dps: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance metrics
Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets
Implement a version-controlled repository for performance baselines and calibration updates
Develop a ruleset to incorporate safety plans and operational policies into performance requirements
selected_dp.selected_dp: Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets

potential_sources: Tight integration between worksite input processing and performance target derivation
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP maintains clear separation by focusing on traceable mapping, which supports independence and traceability without embedding specific processing or storage mechanisms.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite inputs are well-defined and consistently formatted
Risk that changes in worksite policies may require frequent updates to the mapping framework
Assumes version control and history management are handled in separate DPs or modules
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating history and calibration update management into a distinct DP or IM to maintain modularity and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for integrating and managing data flows and performance targets across the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite input data and system performance metrics

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends performance targets and task assignments based on mapping framework

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as mapping framework logically belongs to system-wide solution integration; low diffusion as most data flows through this module.
Partial containment possible but mapping framework would require extensive data from outside vehicle module, causing high diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not data mapping; containment weak and diffusion high.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic, not mapping framework; very weak containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for the mapping framework as it integrates system-wide inputs and performance targets, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from external sources may cause delays or errors in mapping.
If NGHE Solution module grows too large, risk of reduced modularity and maintainability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision recommended; current DP fits well within NGHE Solution module boundary without becoming implementation-specific.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP clearly addresses the FR by establishing a traceable mapping framework that links worksite inputs to performance targets, fulfilling the requirement to derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data from external sources may cause delays or errors in mapping.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within this IM boundary. Other modules show lower containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the system produces documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintains history of baselines/calibration updates, which can be reviewed and tested.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear since the NGHE Solution module is responsible for integrating and managing data flows and performance targets, aligning well with the FR and DP.

recommended_revisions: No revision recommended; current DP fits well within NGHE Solution module boundary without becoming implementation-specific.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear traceability, feasible verification, and well-contained implementation. The DP addresses the FR effectively, and the NGHE Solution module provides a suitable implementation boundary with low diffusion risk. Although there is a potential dependency risk on external telemetry data, it is noted and manageable. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is sufficiently detailed and stable to proceed without further sub-FR derivation at this time.

assumptions: Telemetry data from external sources will be timely and accurate enough to support mapping functions.
The NGHE Solution module will maintain high containment and low diffusion as designed.
open_risks: Dependency on external telemetry data may cause delays or errors in mapping.
evidence_needed: Verification reports demonstrating traceability of performance targets to worksite inputs.
Testing results confirming containment and diffusion scores of NGHE Solution module.
Risk mitigation plans for telemetry data dependency.
